clause_interactions.description: General liability cap may limit the effective value of indemnification obligations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify whether indemnification is carved out from general liability cap or subject to separate super-cap.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Indemnification becomes meaningless if capped below realistic claim values.

clause_interactions.description: Data breach indemnification may be rendered ineffective by a low general liability cap.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure data breach indemnification has a separate super-cap above the general liability cap.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Data breach costs (notification, remediation, regulatory fines) may far exceed general liability cap.

clause_interactions.description: IP infringement indemnification may be capped by general liability limitation.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: IP indemnification should be carved out from general cap or subject to super-cap.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: IP litigation costs and injunction losses typically exceed standard liability caps.

clause_interactions.description: Consequential damages exclusion may prevent recovery of business losses beyond service credits.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that lost profits from SLA failures are not excluded as consequential damages.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Service credits provide only nominal remedy; real business losses are unrecoverable.

clause_interactions.description: Consequential damages exclusion may conflict with data breach indemnification if breach costs are classified as consequential.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Carve out data breach costs from consequential damages exclusion or define as direct damages.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Data breach notification, remediation, and regulatory fines treated as excluded consequential damages.

clause_interactions.description: Uptime commitments are only meaningful if backed by resolution time targets.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that resolution time targets are consistent with uptime percentage commitments.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: 99.9% uptime allows 8.7 hours annual downtime; resolution time must be shorter than this to be achievable.

clause_interactions.description: Uptime targets need corresponding response time commitments to detect and address issues promptly.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure P1 response time is measured in minutes, not hours, to support uptime targets.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Long response times mean outages are not addressed until uptime target is already breached.

clause_interactions.description: Service credits provide financial enforcement mechanism for uptime commitments.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify credit percentages are meaningful (10-30% of monthly fees) and tiered by severity.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Uptime targets without financial consequences are aspirational, not enforceable.

clause_interactions.description: SLA exclusions reduce the effective uptime commitment by excluding certain downtime events.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify exclusions are reasonable: planned maintenance windows, customer-caused issues. Not: all third-party failures.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Broad exclusions reduce effective uptime from 99.9% to 95% or lower in practice.

clause_interactions.description: Termination for cause must not extinguish confidentiality obligations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify confidentiality survives termination for 3-5 years. Trade secrets indefinitely.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Termination releases party from confidentiality, allowing disclosure of sensitive information.

clause_interactions.description: Termination for convenience needs a structured wind-down period to avoid abrupt service cessation.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify wind-down period (3-12 months) matches service complexity.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Convenience termination without wind-down creates cliff-edge service discontinuity.

clause_interactions.description: Even termination for cause needs orderly wind-down for service continuity.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Wind-down obligation should survive even for-cause termination, though period may be shorter.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: For-cause termination with no wind-down leaves customer without service or data.

clause_interactions.description: Insolvency termination may be triggered but wind-down obligations may be unenforceable against an insolvent entity.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Consider data escrow and alternative arrangements that do not depend on insolvent party cooperation.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Wind-down provisions are meaningless if counter-party cannot perform due to insolvency.

clause_interactions.description: Termination fees effectively limit the practical utility of termination for convenience.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify fees are reasonable (declining scale) and do not negate the termination right.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Excessive termination fees make convenience termination financially impractical.

clause_interactions.description: Change of control termination right supplements assignment restrictions by covering indirect transfers.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Both clauses needed: assignment covers direct transfer; change of control covers indirect via share acquisition.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Assignment restriction alone misses indirect transfers through share purchase or merger.

clause_interactions.description: Mutual confidentiality needs well-defined exceptions to be commercially workable.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify standard exceptions: public domain, prior knowledge, independent development, legally compelled disclosure.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Confidentiality without exceptions may prevent normal business operations and legal compliance.

clause_interactions.description: Residuals clause carves out information retained in unaided memory from confidentiality obligations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify residuals clause excludes trade secrets, personal data, and specific proprietary designs.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Broad residuals effectively nullify confidentiality for non-documented information.

clause_interactions.description: Audit rights provide verification mechanism for contractual security measures.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify audit scope covers all security measures specified in the DPA.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Security measures are self-assessed without independent verification.

clause_interactions.description: Sub-processor clause must require flow-down of equivalent security measures.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify sub-processor agreements require at least equivalent security measures.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Data processed by sub-processors with lower security standards than primary processor.

clause_interactions.description: Deletion obligations must align with processing scope to ensure all data is addressed.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify deletion covers all categories of data in processing instructions, including backups.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Some data categories omitted from deletion, remaining with processor after contract end.

clause_interactions.description: Breach notification must be supported by security measures that enable detection.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify security measures include monitoring and detection capabilities to identify breaches.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Notification obligation meaningless if provider lacks capability to detect breaches.

clause_interactions.description: Cross-border transfers to non-adequate countries require Standard Contractual Clauses.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify appropriate SCC module executed with completed annexes and Transfer Impact Assessment.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: International transfer without legal mechanism violates GDPR Chapter V.

clause_interactions.description: SCCs require a Transfer Impact Assessment to determine if supplementary measures are needed.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify TIA conducted documenting destination country laws and identified supplementary measures.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: SCCs may be insufficient without supplementary measures identified by TIA.

clause_interactions.description: Where TIA identifies risks, supplementary measures must be implemented alongside SCCs.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify supplementary measures include: encryption, pseudonymisation, or access restrictions as needed.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: SCCs alone may not provide adequate protection against government access in destination country.

clause_interactions.description: Processor-to-processor SCCs also require TIA for compliance.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure TIA covers the specific P2P transfer scenario and destination country assessment.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Sub-processor transfers without TIA may be challenged by supervisory authority.

clause_interactions.description: SCCs require completed TOMs annex specifying technical and organisational measures.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify TOMs annex is completed with specific, not generic, security measures.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Incomplete SCCs without TOMs annex are not legally valid.

clause_interactions.description: SCC sub-processor annex supplements DPA sub-processor provisions for international transfers.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify consistency between DPA sub-processor list and SCC sub-processor annex.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Inconsistent sub-processor lists between DPA and SCCs create compliance gaps.

clause_interactions.description: Data localisation requirements may conflict with cross-border transfer provisions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that data localisation is not undermined by transfer provisions allowing processing elsewhere.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Contract allows data to leave specified jurisdiction despite localisation requirement.

clause_interactions.description: Data subject rights assistance requires clear understanding of processing scope.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Processing instructions should define data categories to enable effective data subject rights response.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Processor cannot locate all relevant data for data subject request without clear processing scope.

clause_interactions.description: DPIA completion requires processor cooperation and information.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify processor has obligation to provide information needed for DPIA within reasonable timeframe.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Incomplete DPIA due to processor non-cooperation.

clause_interactions.description: Standard force majeure needs a termination right to prevent indefinite suspension.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify termination right available if force majeure continues beyond 60-90 days.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Parties trapped in non-performing contract during extended force majeure.

clause_interactions.description: Narrow force majeure provides tighter definition than standard, reducing abuse potential.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Choose narrow or standard based on risk appetite. Narrow better protects against FM abuse.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Standard FM definition may be too broad, allowing provider to avoid performance for predictable events.

clause_interactions.description: Pandemic-specific provision supplements standard FM with explicit pandemic coverage and mitigation.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Post-COVID, verify pandemic is either included with mitigation duties or explicitly addressed.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Unclear pandemic coverage leads to disputes over FM applicability.

clause_interactions.description: Liability exclusion for force majeure events supplements the performance excuse.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify FM liability exclusion does not extend beyond the scope of the FM clause itself.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Liability exclusion broader than FM definition, creating unintended immunity.

clause_interactions.description: IP work product assignment requires clear background IP reservation to define boundary.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Both clauses must exist with consistent definitions. Background IP schedule should be populated.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Without background reservation, broad work product assignment may inadvertently capture pre-existing IP.

clause_interactions.description: Improvements clause supplements work product ownership by addressing enhancements to existing IP.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify improvements clause is consistent with work product ownership allocation.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Improvements may fall into ownership gap between background and foreground IP.

clause_interactions.description: Licence-back enables provider to reuse general methodologies when work product is assigned to customer.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Licence-back should cover only general tools and methodologies, not customer-specific IP.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Without licence-back, provider loses ability to reuse own methodologies. With broad licence-back, customer loses exclusivity.

clause_interactions.description: Non-assertion covenant supplements licence-back by preventing IP claims within the relationship.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify non-assertion covers both licensed and assigned IP within the agreement scope.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Party may assert IP rights despite existing licence arrangement.

clause_interactions.description: Non-infringement warranty is only meaningful if backed by IP indemnification.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Both warranty and indemnification should exist. Verify indemnification covers full defence costs.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Warranty breach claim exists but without indemnification, customer bears own defence costs.

clause_interactions.description: Fitness warranty needs SLA to define measurable standards for ongoing fitness.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Warranty covers initial delivery; SLA covers ongoing service quality. Both needed for complete coverage.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Gap between warranty period (typically 12 months) and ongoing SLA may leave unaddressed quality period.

clause_interactions.description: Services warranty supplements SLA by setting quality standard for manner of service delivery.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Warranty addresses how services are performed; SLA addresses measurable outcomes. Both needed.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: SLA measures outcomes but not quality of delivery process, or vice versa.

clause_interactions.description: Cyber insurance provides financial backing for data breach super-cap liability.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify insurance coverage equals or exceeds super-cap amount for data protection claims.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Super-cap liability exceeds insurance coverage, leaving gap in financial backing.

clause_interactions.description: Professional indemnity insurance provides financial backing for general liability cap.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify PI coverage at least matches the general liability cap amount.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Liability cap exceeds PI coverage, meaning provider cannot actually pay claims up to cap.

clause_interactions.description: General liability insurance provides financial backing for indemnification obligations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify insurance covers third-party claims that indemnification obligations address.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Indemnification obligations exist but provider lacks insurance to back them.

clause_interactions.description: Security audit rights provide verification mechanism for DPA security measures.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify security audit scope covers all TOMs specified in the DPA.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Security measures claimed but never independently verified.

clause_interactions.description: Regulatory audit rights supplement DPA-specific audit rights for broader compliance verification.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Both general regulatory and DPA-specific audit rights should be included.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: DPA audit rights may be narrower than regulatory requirements demand.

clause_interactions.description: Financial audit rights verify accuracy of usage-based billing.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: For variable pricing, financial audit must cover usage metrics and calculation methodology.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Usage-based billing is unverifiable without audit rights over usage data.

clause_interactions.description: Operational audit rights allow verification of SLA measurement and reporting accuracy.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify operational audit can access SLA measurement tools and underlying data.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: SLA compliance self-reported by provider without independent verification.

clause_interactions.description: Governing law choice should be consistent with dispute resolution mechanism.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify arbitration seat is compatible with governing law for procedural efficiency.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Governing law and arbitration seat in different jurisdictions creates procedural complexity.

clause_interactions.description: Governing law should be paired with a venue clause for completeness.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify venue is consistent with governing law and practical for both parties.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Governing law in one jurisdiction, venue in another creates confusion and expense.

clause_interactions.description: Escalation procedure supplements mediation by providing structured pre-mediation resolution attempt.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify escalation tiers have time limits to prevent indefinite delay before mediation.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Without time limits, escalation becomes delay tactic before mediation can begin.

clause_interactions.description: Mediation provides cost-effective resolution attempt before arbitration.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify mediation has time limit (30-60 days) so it does not delay access to arbitration.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Mandatory mediation without time limit delays arbitration indefinitely.

clause_interactions.description: Litigation and arbitration clauses may conflict if both are specified without clear hierarchy.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Choose one forum. If both referenced, specify clear hierarchy and scope for each.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Parallel proceedings in different forums with potentially inconsistent outcomes.

clause_interactions.description: Affiliate assignment exception supplements general consent requirement.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify affiliate exception requires affiliate to meet same qualification standards.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Broad affiliate exception allows assignment to minimally qualified group company.

clause_interactions.description: Employee-specific non-solicitation provides more targeted protection than general mutual provision.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify scope covers directly involved personnel and period is reasonable (12 months).
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: General mutual non-solicitation may be too broad or too narrow for practical protection.

clause_interactions.description: Authority to contract requires compliance with applicable laws to be meaningful.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Both representations needed. Authority confirms capacity; compliance confirms legality.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Party has authority to contract but may be doing so in violation of applicable law.

clause_interactions.description: Accuracy of information representations are reinforced by corresponding warranty.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Warranty extends liability for inaccurate representations beyond contract execution.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Representation without warranty may limit remedies for inaccurate information.

clause_interactions.description: Anti-bribery compliance requires sanctions screening for effective compliance programme.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Both provisions needed. Anti-bribery addresses conduct; sanctions addresses counter-party status.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Anti-bribery compliance without sanctions screening misses entity-level risk.

clause_interactions.description: Export controls supplement sanctions by addressing technology and goods transfer restrictions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: For technology contracts, both export controls and sanctions compliance needed.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Sanctions compliance without export controls may miss technology transfer restrictions.

clause_interactions.description: DORA service description must include data processing and storage locations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify service description specifies all data locations including sub-processor locations.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Service description incomplete under DORA without data location transparency.

clause_interactions.description: DORA exit strategy must be supported by continuity arrangements during transition.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Exit strategy must include service continuity during transition period.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Exit strategy exists but service continuity during transition is not guaranteed.

clause_interactions.description: DORA audit rights supplement security testing by providing broader oversight capability.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Audit rights should enable verification of security testing results and methodology.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Security testing conducted but results not verifiable through audit.

clause_interactions.description: DORA subcontracting provisions require audit rights to extend to sub-contractors.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify audit rights explicitly cover sub-contractors providing critical or important functions.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Subcontracting permitted but no ability to audit sub-contractor compliance.

clause_interactions.description: Incident reporting must be supported by continuity arrangements to maintain reporting during incidents.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify incident reporting capability is maintained during service disruption.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Major incident disrupts both service and incident reporting capability.

clause_interactions.description: NIS2 incident notification supplements GDPR breach notification for dual-regulated entities.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify notification process addresses both NIS2 (CSIRT) and GDPR (supervisory authority) timelines.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Meeting one notification requirement may not satisfy the other.

clause_interactions.description: NIS2 security requirements include vulnerability handling and patching as core obligations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Patching SLAs should be aligned with NIS2 security requirements (critical within 48 hours).
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: General security requirements without specific patching obligations fail NIS2 compliance.

clause_interactions.description: Supply chain transparency requires access control to prevent unauthorised supply chain changes.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Access controls should prevent unauthorised changes to supply chain components.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Supply chain transparency undermined by inadequate access controls.

clause_interactions.description: Security certification provides third-party validation of NIS2 security requirements.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Certification should cover scope aligned with NIS2 requirements, not just generic security.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Certification scope does not cover all NIS2-required security measures.

clause_interactions.description: HIPAA safeguards must include breach detection capability to trigger notification obligations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Safeguards should include monitoring to detect PHI breaches that trigger notification.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Breach notification obligation exists but no capability to detect qualifying breaches.

clause_interactions.description: HIPAA termination provisions address material breach of BAA including notification failures.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify termination rights cover failure to comply with breach notification obligations.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: No termination remedy for persistent HIPAA breach notification failures.

clause_interactions.description: HIPAA permitted use restrictions may be more restrictive than general DPA processing instructions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: For dual GDPR-HIPAA scope, verify processing instructions satisfy both frameworks.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Processing permitted under GDPR DPA but prohibited under HIPAA permitted uses.

clause_interactions.description: Unlimited carve-outs remove specific risks from the general liability cap.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify carve-outs are appropriate: typically IP infringement, confidentiality breach, wilful misconduct, death/personal injury.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Too many carve-outs effectively negate the liability cap. Too few leave critical risks under-protected.

clause_interactions.description: Data protection super-cap provides elevated but still capped liability for data breaches.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Super-cap typically 2-5x base cap. Verify it covers regulatory fines, notification costs, and remediation.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Super-cap too close to base cap provides no meaningful additional protection for data claims.

clause_interactions.description: Liability floor prevents cap from becoming trivially small for low-value or variable-fee contracts.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Floor should be set at minimum 6-12 months of expected fees.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Without floor, cap tied to variable fees may be negligibly small.

clause_interactions.description: Mutual vs asymmetric structure determines how the liability cap applies to each party.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify whether cap applies equally or asymmetrically and whether asymmetry is justified by risk profile.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Asymmetric cap without commercial justification indicates power imbalance.

clause_interactions.description: Aggregate vs per-incident structure determines how cap accumulates across multiple claims.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify both aggregate annual cap and per-incident cap are defined.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Per-incident only: cumulative claims exceed expectations. Aggregate only: single large claim consumes all capacity.

clause_interactions.description: Financial service credit mechanism provides detailed calculation methodology for SLA credits.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Credit mechanism should define: calculation formula, request process, application timing, and cap.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: SLA credits defined but calculation mechanism missing or unclear.

clause_interactions.description: Liquidated damages provide pre-agreed remedy for SLA failures beyond service credits.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify LDs are genuine pre-estimate of loss to avoid penalty clause risk.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: LDs set too high may be unenforceable as penalties; too low provides no incentive.

clause_interactions.description: Step-in rights provide alternative to termination for critical service failures.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Step-in should be available as remedy before termination, not only after.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Only option is termination, with no intermediate remedy for service failure.

clause_interactions.description: Escrow arrangement provides data and software protection against provider insolvency.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify escrow triggers include insolvency, cessation of support, and material uncured breach.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Insolvency termination right exists but data/software is inaccessible without escrow.

clause_interactions.description: Transition pricing defines costs during wind-down period.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Transition pricing should be at prevailing or slightly reduced rates, not punitive rates.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Wind-down obligation exists but at inflated pricing that penalises exit.

clause_interactions.description: Stranded costs provisions supplement termination fees for contract-specific investments.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify stranded costs are amortised and recoverable on early termination, not duplicative of termination fees.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Customer pays both termination fees and unamortised stranded costs, creating double penalty.

clause_interactions.description: Price lock period may conflict with CPI adjustment if both apply simultaneously.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify price lock takes precedence during lock period, with CPI adjustment only after lock expires.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Ambiguity about whether CPI adjustment applies during price lock period.

clause_interactions.description: Most-favoured customer clause supplements benchmarking by providing automatic price protection.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: MFC provides real-time protection; benchmarking provides periodic market comparison. Both are valuable.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Benchmarking without MFC means customer only discovers overpricing periodically.

clause_interactions.description: Late payment interest provides incentive for timely payment within net-30 terms.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Interest rate should be commercially reasonable (base rate + 2-4%).
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Net-30 terms without late interest incentive leads to systematic late payment.

clause_interactions.description: Open book accounting provides ongoing transparency supplementing periodic cost verification.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Open book should cover all cost categories used in pricing calculations.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Cost verification right without access to underlying data is difficult to exercise.

clause_interactions.description: Parent guarantee provides broader financial backing complementing specific performance bond.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: One or both may be appropriate depending on counter-party financial strength.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Neither guarantee nor bond means contractual obligations have no financial backing.

clause_interactions.description: Gain-sharing incentivises continuous improvement complementing benchmarking's market alignment.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Gain-sharing motivates efficiency; benchmarking ensures market alignment. Both drive value.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Benchmarking without gain-sharing means provider has no incentive to drive efficiencies.

clause_interactions.description: Net-30 payment terms need late interest to incentivise compliance.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify interest clause specifies: rate, start date, compounding, and application process.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Payment terms without interest incentive are routinely exceeded.

clause_interactions.description: Price adjustment mechanism supplemented by MFC to prevent adjustments above market.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: MFC should cap adjustments at rates offered to comparable customers.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Price adjustments may exceed market rates without MFC protection.

clause_interactions.description: Customer and provider indemnification may conflict when both parties contributed to a loss.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Include contributory fault mechanism to apportion liability for joint-cause incidents.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Mutual claims with no apportionment mechanism leads to litigation.

clause_interactions.description: DPA personnel confidentiality supplements general confidentiality for data protection compliance.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify DPA confidentiality covers all personnel processing personal data, including temporary staff.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: General confidentiality may not meet GDPR Article 28(3)(b) specific personnel requirement.

clause_interactions.description: Records of processing provide evidence trail for processing instruction compliance.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: ROPA should be consistent with and traceable to documented processing instructions.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Processing records do not match instructions, indicating unauthorised processing.

clause_interactions.description: Title warranty provides assurance that IP assignment is valid and unencumbered.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Title warranty should confirm: clear ownership, no encumbrances, no prior assignments or licences.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: IP assigned without title warranty may be subject to prior claims.

clause_interactions.description: Warranty of compliance extends representation into ongoing obligation throughout contract term.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Representation covers execution date; warranty covers ongoing performance period. Both needed.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Representation only covers execution date, leaving ongoing compliance unaddressed.

clause_interactions.description: Milestone payments need acceptance criteria to determine when payment is earned.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Each milestone should have defined acceptance criteria and testing period before payment is triggered.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Payment triggered by milestone completion without quality verification.

clause_interactions.description: Additional insured status provides customer direct access to provider's insurance coverage.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify additional insured endorsement on policy, not just contractual commitment.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Contractual commitment to add as additional insured but no actual policy endorsement.

clause_interactions.description: Penalty cap limits maximum accumulation of liquidated damages.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify penalty cap is reasonable (10-20% of affected deliverable value) and includes termination right at cap.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: No cap means liquidated damages can accumulate beyond contract value.

clause_interactions.description: Earn-back mechanism supplements service credits by incentivising provider improvement.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Earn-back should require sustained above-target performance (3+ months) before credit recovery.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Without earn-back, provider may prefer termination over continuous credit outflow.

clause_interactions.description: AI liability allocation clauses depend on explainability provisions to determine fault attribution when an AI system causes harm or produces erroneous outputs. Without explainability, proving causation is effectively impossible.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure explainability obligations are sufficient to support the liability framework. Verify that the party bearing liability has access to model explanations.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Party allocated liability cannot investigate or contest fault due to opaque AI decision-making.

clause_interactions.description: Ownership of AI outputs may conflict with training data rights when outputs are substantially derived from or incorporate patterns learned from the training data provided by the other party.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Clarify whether outputs derived from one party's training data create joint or derivative IP rights. Address output ownership carve-outs for training-data-heavy outputs.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Disputed ownership of AI-generated work product where training data contributed materially to the output.

clause_interactions.description: Human oversight provisions supplement automated decision-making clauses by establishing review checkpoints, escalation thresholds, and override procedures for AI-driven decisions that impact individuals.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that human oversight scope covers all automated decisions identified in the contract, particularly those with legal or significant effects on data subjects.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Automated decisions proceed without required human review, creating regulatory exposure under EU AI Act and GDPR Article 22.

clause_interactions.description: Bias monitoring obligations depend on algorithmic audit capabilities to detect, measure, and report on discriminatory patterns in AI model outputs across protected characteristics.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure audit scope explicitly includes bias detection methodologies, fairness metrics, and demographic parity testing requirements.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Bias monitoring obligations exist on paper but lack enforcement mechanism through auditing.

clause_interactions.description: Model governance frameworks require versioning to track model changes, maintain audit trails, enable rollback to previous versions, and satisfy regulatory requirements for model lifecycle management.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Confirm versioning includes semantic version numbering, change documentation, performance comparison against prior versions, and retention of deprecated versions.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Governance framework cannot track which model version caused an incident or satisfy regulatory inquiries about model lineage.

clause_interactions.description: Liability provisions for AI hallucinations depend on content filtering mechanisms to mitigate the frequency and severity of fabricated outputs before they reach end users.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify content filtering covers factual accuracy checking, citation verification, and confidence scoring. Ensure filtering SLAs align with liability thresholds.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Party bears hallucination liability without adequate technical safeguards to prevent hallucinated outputs.

clause_interactions.description: General liability caps may limit the effective protection of AI output indemnification, particularly where AI-generated content causes IP infringement or defamation claims exceeding the cap amount.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Consider whether AI output indemnity should be carved out from the general liability cap or subject to a separate AI-specific super-cap.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: AI output indemnity is capped at an amount insufficient to cover potential IP infringement or defamation claims from AI-generated content.

clause_interactions.description: Training restrictions constrain the scope of model governance by limiting which data can be used for retraining, fine-tuning, or model improvement, potentially preventing necessary model updates.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure training restrictions are compatible with model governance requirements for continuous improvement, bias correction, and security patching.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Overly restrictive training limitations prevent necessary model improvements mandated by governance framework.

clause_interactions.description: Vendor lock-in provisions that restrict migration or interoperability directly conflict with data portability requirements that mandate the ability to export and transfer AI models, configurations, and data.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Negotiate data portability carve-outs within vendor lock-in clauses. Ensure export formats are industry-standard and migration assistance is included.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Customer trapped with underperforming AI vendor due to inability to port data and models despite contractual portability rights.

clause_interactions.description: Responsible AI commitments require operational bias monitoring to ensure fairness principles are actively enforced rather than remaining aspirational policy statements.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Map responsible AI principles to specific, measurable bias monitoring KPIs. Ensure monitoring frequency and scope match the responsible AI commitments.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Responsible AI obligations become marketing statements without operational bias detection and remediation processes.

clause_interactions.description: Model deprecation provisions require cloud exit planning to ensure customers can migrate workloads when a model version is end-of-lifed, particularly for cloud-hosted AI services.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify deprecation notice periods align with cloud exit timelines. Ensure deprecated model alternatives are identified before exit planning is triggered.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Model deprecation forces emergency migration without adequate exit plan, causing service disruption.

clause_interactions.description: AI SaaS performance commitments depend on API-level SLAs to define measurable availability, latency, and throughput targets for the AI service endpoints.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure API SLAs cover inference latency, batch processing throughput, and error rate thresholds that underpin the SaaS performance commitments.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: SaaS performance promises lack enforceable API-level metrics, making service credit claims unsubstantiable.

clause_interactions.description: Synthetic data provisions carve out training restrictions by permitting the use of artificially generated data for model training even when restrictions exist on using actual customer or proprietary data.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that synthetic data generation methodology preserves the statistical properties needed without memorizing or leaking source data characteristics.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Synthetic data carve-out abused to circumvent training restrictions by generating data too closely derived from restricted sources.

clause_interactions.description: Technology escrow provisions supplement vendor lock-in protections by ensuring source code, model weights, and training pipelines are held by a neutral third party for release upon trigger events.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Confirm escrow materials include complete model artifacts (weights, hyperparameters, training scripts, data pipelines) sufficient to independently operate the AI system.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Escrow provides incomplete model artifacts that cannot be operationalized independently, rendering the lock-in protection illusory.

clause_interactions.description: Multi-tenant AI deployments require data residency provisions to ensure tenant data isolation extends to geographic placement, preventing commingling of data subject to different jurisdictional requirements.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that multi-tenancy architecture enforces tenant-level data residency controls and that model inference does not cross residency boundaries.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Multi-tenant architecture processes data across jurisdictions, violating tenant-specific residency requirements and triggering regulatory non-compliance.

clause_interactions.description: AI-specific penetration testing provisions supplement general audit rights by adding adversarial testing of model endpoints, prompt injection resistance, and data extraction vulnerability assessment.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure penetration testing scope includes model-specific attacks (adversarial inputs, prompt injection, training data extraction) beyond standard infrastructure testing.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Audit rights cover infrastructure but miss AI-specific attack vectors, leaving model vulnerabilities undiscovered.

clause_interactions.description: IP rights in prompt engineering may conflict with output ownership when sophisticated prompts are the primary creative input producing valuable outputs, creating competing ownership claims.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Clarify whether prompt engineering IP conveys derivative rights in outputs. Define ownership boundaries between prompt templates and generated content.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Both parties claim output ownership based on competing theories of creative contribution (prompt design vs. model capability).

clause_interactions.description: Usage metrics provide the measurement infrastructure needed to verify SaaS performance commitments, including token consumption, inference counts, and processing time tracking.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure usage metrics are granular enough to validate performance SLAs and are accessible to the customer for independent verification.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Performance commitments cannot be verified due to insufficient or inaccessible usage metrics.

clause_interactions.description: Foundation model license terms may restrict how training data can be used with the model, including fine-tuning limitations, output usage restrictions, and competitive use prohibitions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Cross-reference foundation model license restrictions against training data usage plans. Verify fine-tuning rights are explicitly granted.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Training data rights exist but cannot be exercised due to foundation model license restrictions on fine-tuning or derivative model creation.

clause_interactions.description: Explainability provisions supplement automated decision-making by providing the technical means to generate human-understandable explanations for individual decisions, supporting regulatory compliance and dispute resolution.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify explainability methods produce decision-specific explanations (not just general model descriptions) suitable for the decision's impact level.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Automated decisions lack individual explanations required for regulatory compliance or customer disputes.

clause_interactions.description: Variation orders depend on the interim payment mechanism to ensure instructed changes are valued and paid within the established payment cycle rather than deferred to final account.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure variation valuation rules are incorporated into interim payment applications and that the payment mechanism accommodates provisional sums for instructed variations.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Contractor performs varied work without interim payment, creating cash flow pressure and potential suspension rights.

clause_interactions.description: Extensions of time reduce or eliminate the employer's entitlement to delay damages by establishing that delays were caused by employer risk events, neutral events, or qualifying causes.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that granted extensions of time automatically adjust the delay damages calculation period. Check for concurrent delay provisions.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Delay damages applied to periods where contractor was entitled to time extension, creating unjust enrichment for the employer.

clause_interactions.description: Practical completion triggers the defects liability period during which the contractor must remedy defects appearing in the works. The defects liability mechanism depends on a clear practical completion date.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Confirm that practical completion certification triggers defects liability start date and that the period length is consistent across the contract.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Defects liability period start date is unclear, creating disputes about remediation obligations and retention release timing.

clause_interactions.description: Retention provides financial security for the defects liability period, giving the employer funds to remedy defects if the contractor fails to do so within the rectification period.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify retention release schedule aligns with defects liability period end. Check whether half is released at practical completion and the remainder at defects liability expiry.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Retention released before defects liability expires, removing the employer's financial security for defect remediation.

clause_interactions.description: Performance bonds provide additional financial security beyond retention to cover delay damages claims, ensuring the employer can recover losses from late completion even if the contractor becomes insolvent.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure performance bond value covers anticipated delay damages exposure. Verify bond validity period extends beyond expected completion date.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Performance bond expires or is insufficient to cover delay damages, leaving employer exposed on late completion.

clause_interactions.description: Contractor design liability obligations require professional indemnity insurance to ensure the contractor can meet claims arising from design errors or omissions in the contractor-designed portions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify PI insurance covers the full scope of contractor design obligations with adequate limits. Confirm policy is maintained for the required survival period.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Design liability exists but contractor lacks PI insurance to fund claims, making design liability obligation unenforceable in practice.

clause_interactions.description: Back-to-back subcontract provisions require sub-processor obligations to flow down when the subcontractor processes personal data, ensuring GDPR compliance through the supply chain.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that back-to-back terms include complete DPA sub-processor requirements and that subcontractors accept equivalent data protection obligations.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Main contractor liable for subcontractor data breaches where sub-processor obligations were not properly flowed down.

clause_interactions.description: Step-in rights provide an alternative to termination at will, allowing the employer to assume control of specific operations without terminating the entire contract.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Clarify the relationship between step-in and termination: does step-in defer termination rights, or can both be exercised simultaneously?
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Employer terminates at will when step-in would have preserved project continuity at lower cost and disruption.

clause_interactions.description: Pay-when-paid prohibition under construction legislation directly conflicts with any prompt payment provisions that condition payment timing on receipt of upstream payments in the supply chain.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Remove all conditional payment language from subcontracts. Ensure prompt payment obligations are absolute and comply with the Construction Act.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Unenforceable payment terms that violate statutory prohibition, exposing main contractor to adjudication claims.

clause_interactions.description: Collateral warranties extend design liability to third parties (funders, tenants, purchasers) who are not parties to the building contract, creating direct contractual relationships for design claims.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure collateral warranty design liability standard matches the building contract (reasonable skill and care vs. fitness for purpose). Check step-in provisions.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Collateral warranty imposes stricter design standard than building contract, creating uninsurable fitness-for-purpose liability.

clause_interactions.description: Parent company guarantees provide additional security alongside performance bonds, particularly valuable where the contractor is a special purpose vehicle or thinly capitalised subsidiary.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify parent company financial strength. Check guarantee scope covers all contract obligations including defects liability, not just completion.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Performance bond alone insufficient where contractor entity has limited assets and parent company guarantee was not obtained.

clause_interactions.description: Sectional completion provisions require section-specific delay damages rates to ensure the employer can recover losses attributable to late completion of individual sections.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify each section has its own delay damages rate proportionate to the loss flowing from that section's late completion. Check that rates do not overlap.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Single delay damages rate applied to sectional completion creates penalty risk or under-recovery depending on which section is delayed.

clause_interactions.description: Loss and expense claims supplement variation order provisions by covering the disruption and prolongation costs arising from variations that are not captured in the variation valuation itself.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Clarify whether variation valuations include disruption costs or whether these must be claimed separately as loss and expense.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Contractor under-recovered on variations because disruption costs were neither included in variation valuation nor claimable as loss and expense.

clause_interactions.description: The statutory right to suspend performance depends on the interim payment mechanism because suspension is triggered by non-payment of sums due under the payment provisions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure interim payment provisions comply with the Construction Act to preserve the contractor's valid suspension right. Check notice requirements.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Suspension exercised without proper payment mechanism creates disputed entitlement and potential wrongful suspension claim.

clause_interactions.description: Contractor's all-risks insurance supplements practical completion by covering physical damage to the works until handover, when risk typically transfers to the employer.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify CAR insurance cover period extends to actual practical completion date plus any extension of time. Check gap coverage for delayed completion.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: CAR insurance expires before practical completion, leaving works uninsured during the final construction phase.

clause_interactions.description: CDM Regulations compliance in construction contracts requires health and safety provisions for all workers on site, establishing the principal contractor's and designer's duties under the regulations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify health and safety obligations cover CDM-specific duties including construction phase plan, welfare facilities, and competency requirements.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: CDM compliance obligations exist but health and safety provisions are insufficient to discharge them, exposing all duty holders.

clause_interactions.description: Building Safety Act obligations supplement design liability by imposing additional duties on accountable persons and duty holders for higher-risk buildings throughout the building lifecycle.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure design liability extends to Building Safety Act golden thread requirements and that information management obligations are allocated.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Design liability does not cover Building Safety Act duties, creating regulatory gaps for higher-risk buildings.

clause_interactions.description: Advance payment guarantees provide security for mobilisation payments made before work commences, ensuring recovery if the contractor fails to perform after receiving advance funds.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify guarantee value matches advance payment amount and that repayment schedule through interim certificates is clearly defined.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Advance payment made without guarantee, leaving employer exposed if contractor becomes insolvent before work commences.

clause_interactions.description: Final account settlement requires complete variation order valuation because all instructed changes must be agreed and incorporated into the final contract sum calculation.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure all variations are valued and agreed before final account submission. Check time bar provisions for variation claims.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Final account disputed due to unresolved variation valuations, delaying project close-out and retention release.

clause_interactions.description: Sustainability requirements in construction contracts supplement modern slavery provisions by addressing broader social value obligations including fair wages, skills training, and local employment targets.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify sustainability requirements include specific modern slavery due diligence obligations for the construction supply chain.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Sustainability framework addresses environmental aspects but omits social/labour standards enforcement.

clause_interactions.description: Non-compete restrictions are more likely enforceable when supported by garden leave provisions that provide continued remuneration during the restricted period, addressing proportionality concerns.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure garden leave duration counts toward the non-compete period. Verify the combined restriction period is reasonable for the seniority level.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Non-compete enforceability challenged on proportionality grounds where no garden leave pay is provided during the restriction.

clause_interactions.description: Non-solicitation provisions supplement non-compete clauses by providing an alternative, less restrictive protection where full non-compete may be unenforceable or unnecessary.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Consider whether non-solicitation alone provides sufficient protection, making a broader non-compete unnecessary and potentially unenforceable.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Overly broad non-compete struck down by court while narrower non-solicitation would have been upheld and adequate.

clause_interactions.description: IP assignment clauses depend on confidentiality obligations to protect the assigned intellectual property from disclosure, particularly during the development phase before formal IP registration.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify confidentiality scope covers all IP categories assigned (inventions, designs, software, know-how) and that survival period exceeds IP registration timelines.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Assigned IP disclosed to competitors before registration or commercial exploitation due to inadequate confidentiality protection.

clause_interactions.description: Stock option vesting schedules may conflict with good/bad leaver provisions in shareholder agreements, particularly regarding accelerated vesting on change of control versus forfeiture on termination.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Reconcile vesting acceleration triggers with leaver classification criteria. Ensure employment agreement and shareholder agreement are consistent.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Employee classified as bad leaver loses unvested options despite vesting acceleration clause in employment agreement, creating litigation risk.

clause_interactions.description: Severance payments supplement notice period entitlements by providing additional compensation beyond the notice period, typically in exchange for enhanced restrictive covenants or settlement of claims.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Clarify whether severance is in addition to or inclusive of notice pay. Verify severance conditions (e.g., signing a release) are clearly stated.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Dispute over whether severance includes or is additional to notice pay, leading to under-payment claims.

clause_interactions.description: Restrictive covenants require clear post-termination provisions defining when restrictions commence, their duration, and the obligations of both parties during the restricted period.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure post-termination provisions specify the start date, duration, geographic scope, and any payment obligations during the restricted period.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Restrictive covenants unenforceable due to ambiguous commencement date or duration of post-termination restrictions.

clause_interactions.description: TUPE transfer provisions require redundancy clauses because post-transfer restructuring may necessitate redundancies, which must comply with both TUPE protections and redundancy consultation requirements.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that redundancy provisions comply with TUPE restrictions on transfer-connected dismissals. Include ETO (economic, technical, organisational) reason analysis.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Post-transfer redundancies deemed automatically unfair because redundancy provisions failed to account for TUPE protections.

clause_interactions.description: Whistleblower protection provisions supplement grievance procedures by providing an alternative reporting channel with enhanced protections against retaliation for qualifying disclosures.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure whistleblower channel is independent from standard grievance process. Verify anti-retaliation protections exceed standard grievance protections.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Whistleblowers forced to use standard grievance channel lacking retaliation protections, deterring legitimate disclosures.

clause_interactions.description: Discretionary bonus provisions may conflict with clawback clauses where the employer exercises discretion to award a bonus but later seeks to recover it, particularly if clawback triggers are ambiguous.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define clawback triggers precisely and ensure they apply regardless of the discretionary nature of the bonus award. Address tax treatment of clawed-back amounts.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Clawback of discretionary bonus challenged as penalty or breach of implied trust and confidence.

clause_interactions.description: Remote work provisions require health and safety clauses covering the employer's duty of care to employees working from home, including workstation assessment, equipment provision, and accident reporting.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify health and safety obligations extend to the remote work environment. Include display screen equipment assessment and home office risk assessment requirements.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Employer liable for home workplace injuries where health and safety obligations did not extend to the remote work location.

clause_interactions.description: Probation period provisions typically limit notice period entitlements by establishing shorter notice requirements during the initial employment period, with full notice rights commencing after probation.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify probation notice periods comply with statutory minimums. Ensure probation extension provisions clearly state applicable notice terms.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Reduced probation notice period falls below statutory minimum, making the short notice provision void.

clause_interactions.description: Pension contribution provisions supplement severance by clarifying whether employer pension contributions continue during garden leave and whether severance payments are pensionable.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Specify whether severance payments attract employer pension contributions and whether auto-enrolment obligations apply during the notice/garden leave period.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Dispute over pension treatment of severance payments, potentially increasing severance cost by employer contribution percentage.

clause_interactions.description: Commission structure provisions require clear termination clauses to address payment of pipeline commissions, clawback of paid commissions, and treatment of commissions earned but not yet payable at termination.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define commission entitlement for deals in pipeline at termination. Address whether commission survives termination for cause versus without cause.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Post-termination commission disputes where pipeline deals close after departure but were substantially developed during employment.

clause_interactions.description: Training repayment clauses limit an employee's ability to resign freely by imposing financial obligations for repayment of training costs if the employee leaves before a specified period expires.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure repayment amounts are proportionate (diminishing over time) and that repayment is not triggered by employer-initiated termination or constructive dismissal.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Training repayment clause deemed penalty or restraint of trade due to disproportionate amount or unreasonable duration.

clause_interactions.description: Flexible working provisions supplement remote work policies by covering broader arrangements including compressed hours, job sharing, and flextime that may combine with remote work arrangements.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure flexible working requests process covers remote work as one option among broader flexibility arrangements. Address core hours and availability requirements.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Remote work policy treated as separate from flexible working framework, creating inconsistent entitlements and approval processes.

clause_interactions.description: Employee data subject access rights require confidentiality provisions to define the boundaries of disclosure, particularly regarding third-party personal data and legal privilege within employee records.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure confidentiality carve-outs permit lawful data subject access responses. Address redaction procedures for third-party data within requested records.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Confidentiality obligations invoked to unlawfully restrict employee data subject access requests.

clause_interactions.description: Equal pay obligations require transparent bonus discretion criteria to demonstrate that pay differentials (including discretionary bonuses) are not based on protected characteristics.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure bonus discretion criteria are documented, consistently applied, and capable of justification in equal pay audit. Track bonus awards by demographic data.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Discretionary bonus awards create equal pay liability where criteria are opaque and outcomes show demographic disparities.

clause_interactions.description: Employee retention bonuses triggered by change of control supplement M&A change of control provisions by incentivising key personnel to remain during the transition period.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify retention bonus triggers align with the M&A change of control definition. Ensure retention period extends beyond expected integration timeline.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Retention bonus triggers misaligned with actual change of control mechanics, either paying too early or failing to trigger when needed.

clause_interactions.description: Moonlighting permissions may conflict with non-compete obligations where permitted secondary employment overlaps with the employer's competitive landscape or where moonlighting exceptions undermine non-compete scope.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure moonlighting approval process considers non-compete scope. Define what constitutes competing activity for both moonlighting and non-compete purposes.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Approved moonlighting activity inadvertently waives non-compete by establishing employer tolerance of competitive work.

clause_interactions.description: Background check provisions require data subject access rights to ensure candidates and employees can access, verify, and challenge the results of pre-employment and ongoing screening processes.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure background check consent forms include data subject rights information. Define dispute resolution for contested background check findings.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Adverse employment decisions based on erroneous background check data that the employee could not access or challenge.

clause_interactions.description: Earn-out provisions require information rights to enable the seller to verify earn-out calculations, monitor target performance, and challenge buyer manipulation of earn-out metrics.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure information rights include access to financial records, management accounts, and operating metrics relevant to earn-out calculation.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Seller unable to verify earn-out calculations, creating opportunity for buyer manipulation of post-completion performance metrics.

clause_interactions.description: Material adverse change clauses conflict with locked-box pricing mechanisms because MAC protection implies price adjustment for post-signing changes, while locked box fixes the price at a historical date.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: If using locked box, limit MAC to narrow existential events. Consider whether MAC walkaway right is appropriate when economic risk passes at locked box date.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: MAC clause undermines locked-box certainty by reintroducing price uncertainty that locked box was designed to eliminate.

clause_interactions.description: Representations and warranties require a disclosure letter to qualify warranty statements with known exceptions, preventing warranty claims for matters fairly disclosed to the buyer before completion.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure disclosure letter is specific and detailed. Reject general disclosures that effectively hollow out warranty protection. Set materiality thresholds for disclosures.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Buyer faces warranty claims for known issues that should have been disclosed, or seller faces claims despite good faith disclosure of known matters.

clause_interactions.description: De minimis and basket thresholds limit specific indemnity claims by establishing minimum claim values and aggregate thresholds before the indemnifying party becomes liable.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Negotiate whether specific indemnities fall inside or outside the basket mechanism. Critical indemnities (tax, environmental) should typically be carved out from basket.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Specific indemnity protection undermined by high basket thresholds that prevent legitimate but individually small claims from being recovered.

clause_interactions.description: Escrow arrangements supplement warranty protection by holding a portion of the purchase price in trust to satisfy warranty claims, ensuring the seller has funds available for claims.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Align escrow release schedule with warranty claim limitation periods. Ensure escrow amount is sufficient to cover anticipated warranty exposure.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Escrow released before warranty claims crystallise, removing the buyer's primary recovery mechanism for warranty breaches.

clause_interactions.description: Warranty and indemnity insurance supplements warranty protection by providing a third-party insurer as claims respondent, reducing friction between buyer and seller on warranty claims.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify W&I policy covers all material warranties. Check policy exclusions against known risk areas. Ensure no-claims declaration aligns with disclosure letter.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: W&I insurance excludes key warranty areas or fails to respond to claims due to non-disclosure at policy inception.

clause_interactions.description: Conditions precedent require completion accounts methodology to be agreed before signing, ensuring both parties understand how the price adjustment mechanism will operate at completion.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define completion accounts preparation methodology, dispute resolution process, and accounting policies as conditions precedent or pre-completion deliverables.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Completion delayed by disputes over completion accounts methodology that should have been agreed as a condition precedent.

clause_interactions.description: Drag-along rights allowing majority shareholders to force minority sales may conflict with tag-along rights designed to protect minority shareholders from being left behind in a partial exit.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Clarify priority between drag-along and tag-along in exit scenarios. Define threshold at which drag-along overrides tag-along protections.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Minority shareholders face forced sale on unfavourable terms where tag-along protections are overridden by drag-along mechanics.

clause_interactions.description: Post-acquisition non-compete provisions require earn-out alignment because seller competition would directly undermine the earn-out calculation and destroy the basis for deferred consideration.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure non-compete duration covers the entire earn-out measurement period. Define competitive activities by reference to the target business scope.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Seller competes during earn-out period, reducing target performance and earn-out payments, with no contractual recourse.

clause_interactions.description: Contractual limitation periods constrain the time within which warranty claims must be brought, potentially barring legitimate claims discovered after the limitation period expires.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Set different limitation periods for different warranty categories: tax (7 years), general business (2-3 years), title (indefinite). Align with W&I insurance policy period.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Warranty claims time-barred before the buyer could reasonably have discovered the breach, particularly for tax and environmental warranties.

clause_interactions.description: Reserved matters supplement shareholders agreement protections by specifying decisions requiring unanimous or supermajority consent, preventing majority shareholders from taking unilateral material actions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure reserved matters list covers all material decisions including capital expenditure, key hires, related party transactions, and business strategy changes.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Minority investors lack veto rights on material decisions not covered by reserved matters, despite SHA protection intention.

clause_interactions.description: Put and call option provisions require a valuation mechanism (often referencing completion accounts methodology) to determine the exercise price when the option is triggered.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define the valuation methodology, timing, and dispute resolution for option exercise price. Consider whether fair market value or formula-based pricing applies.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Option triggered without agreed valuation mechanism, leading to protracted dispute over exercise price.

clause_interactions.description: Anti-dilution provisions supplement pre-emption rights by providing price-based protection against dilutive issuances, whereas pre-emption provides volume-based protection through participation rights.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Clarify interaction between anti-dilution adjustment and pre-emption exercise. Define whether anti-dilution is full ratchet or weighted average.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Investor protected against dilution on volume (pre-emption) but not on price (anti-dilution), or vice versa.

clause_interactions.description: Key person provisions require management warranties to ensure identified key individuals are bound by appropriate employment terms, non-competes, and IP assignments that protect the acquired business.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify management warranties cover key persons' employment status, restrictive covenants, and any side arrangements. Check for change of control triggers in key person agreements.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Key persons depart post-acquisition because management warranties did not cover retention mechanisms or restrictive covenant adequacy.

clause_interactions.description: Deadlock resolution mechanisms supplement reserved matters by providing escalation paths when shareholders cannot agree on reserved matter decisions, preventing permanent operational paralysis.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure deadlock mechanism covers all reserved matters. Define deadlock triggers, cooling-off periods, and ultimate resolution (Russian roulette, Texas shoot-out, expert determination).
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Deadlock on reserved matters with no resolution mechanism, causing business paralysis and value destruction.
