clause_interactions.description: IP warranties in M&A transactions require background IP reservation to identify and exclude seller-retained IP from the scope of IP warranties, preventing overreach of warranty protection.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Schedule all background IP excluded from sale. Ensure IP warranties cover only transferred IP and that license-back arrangements are agreed for any shared IP.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: IP warranty scope unclear, creating claims for IP that the seller legitimately retained or that was never intended to transfer.

clause_interactions.description: Tax warranties require appropriate limitation periods aligned with tax authority assessment windows, which are typically longer than general warranty limitation periods.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Set tax warranty limitation period to cover the relevant tax authority's assessment and audit windows (typically 6-7 years). Align with tax deed provisions.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Tax warranty expires before HMRC or equivalent tax authority can raise assessment, leaving buyer exposed to historic tax liabilities.

clause_interactions.description: Employment warranties supplement TUPE transfer provisions by confirming the accuracy of employee liability information, terms of employment, and outstanding tribunal claims before transfer.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify employment warranties cover all ELI requirements. Check for undisclosed employment disputes, redundancy proposals, or collective agreement changes.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: TUPE transfer proceeds without adequate employment warranties, and buyer inherits undisclosed employment liabilities.

clause_interactions.description: Material contracts warranty requires assignment consent analysis because key contracts may contain change of control or anti-assignment provisions that must be addressed before or at completion.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Review all material contracts for change of control triggers and assignment restrictions. Obtain necessary consents as conditions precedent to completion.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Material contracts terminated post-acquisition due to unconsented change of control, destroying acquired business value.

clause_interactions.description: Litigation warranties supplement specific indemnities by providing warranty protection for undisclosed litigation while specific indemnities cover known litigation matters disclosed during due diligence.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure known litigation is covered by specific indemnity (not just warranty) with appropriate caps. Use warranty for unknown/undisclosed claims.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Known litigation covered only by warranty (subject to general limitations) when specific indemnity with dedicated cap was needed.

clause_interactions.description: Incoterms delivery terms require bill of lading provisions to define when risk and responsibility for goods transfer between parties, particularly for CIF, CFR, and FOB terms involving maritime transport.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure bill of lading type (negotiable vs. straight) aligns with the selected Incoterm. Verify document presentation requirements match trade finance arrangements.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Risk transfer point ambiguous due to inconsistency between Incoterms and bill of lading terms, creating cargo insurance gaps.

clause_interactions.description: Letter of credit payment mechanisms require strict documentary compliance provisions because banks pay against documents, not goods, and even minor discrepancies can block payment.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define acceptable document discrepancies and cure periods. Ensure documentary requirements are achievable and consistent with Incoterms and insurance provisions.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Letter of credit payment blocked due to documentary discrepancies that could have been prevented with proper compliance provisions.

clause_interactions.description: Sanctions compliance requirements limit trade finance availability because banks and financial institutions screen all transactions against sanctions lists and may refuse to process payments involving sanctioned parties or territories.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Include sanctions screening provisions in trade finance arrangements. Define consequences of sanctions-related payment blocks and alternative payment mechanisms.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Trade finance facility frozen due to sanctions hit on counterparty, with no alternative payment mechanism agreed.

clause_interactions.description: Choice of law provisions require jurisdiction clauses to ensure the chosen governing law is applied by courts or tribunals with competence to hear disputes, preventing forum shopping.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Align governing law with jurisdiction selection. Consider enforceability of judgments in the counterparty's jurisdiction under bilateral treaties.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Judgment obtained in chosen jurisdiction cannot be enforced in the counterparty's country due to no reciprocal enforcement treaty.

clause_interactions.description: ICC arbitration supplements choice of law by providing a neutral dispute resolution forum that can apply the chosen governing law regardless of either party's domicile, with enforceable awards under the New York Convention.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Specify arbitration seat (not just venue) as this determines the procedural law. Ensure seat country is a New York Convention signatory.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Arbitral award unenforceable because seat selected in non-New York Convention jurisdiction.

clause_interactions.description: Export control regulations limit technology transfer provisions by restricting the transfer of controlled technologies, software, and technical data to certain jurisdictions and end-users.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Classify all transferred technology under applicable export control regimes (EAR, EU Dual-Use Regulation). Obtain necessary export licenses before technology transfer.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Technology transferred in violation of export controls, creating criminal liability and sanctions risk for both parties.

clause_interactions.description: International force majeure provisions supplement hardship clauses by covering impossibility of performance, while hardship addresses situations where performance is possible but economically unreasonable.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define the boundary between force majeure (impossibility) and hardship (excessive burden). Ensure both mechanisms have distinct triggers and remedies.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Gap between force majeure and hardship where performance is extremely difficult but not impossible, leaving the affected party without relief.

clause_interactions.description: Currency hedging provisions supplement international trade terms by allocating and mitigating exchange rate risk between contract signing and payment dates.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define hedging obligations, permitted instruments, and cost allocation. Specify reference exchange rate and adjustment mechanism for long-term contracts.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Exchange rate fluctuation wipes out contract margin where no hedging mechanism was agreed despite multi-currency exposure.

clause_interactions.description: International anti-bribery provisions require local counsel engagement to navigate jurisdiction-specific bribery and corruption laws, facilitation payment rules, and local enforcement practices.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Require local legal counsel sign-off on compliance programme adequacy. Include local counsel review of agent and intermediary arrangements.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Anti-bribery compliance programme fails to address local law nuances, creating exposure despite good faith compliance efforts.

clause_interactions.description: Dual-use goods provisions require customs compliance to ensure proper classification, licensing, end-user certification, and reporting of goods with both civilian and military applications.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify dual-use classification under applicable regulations. Ensure customs declarations reflect controlled status and that end-user certificates are obtained.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Dual-use goods shipped without proper customs declarations and export licenses, creating criminal and sanctions exposure.

clause_interactions.description: Foreign judgment enforcement provisions require careful choice of law selection because enforcement depends on bilateral treaties, reciprocity principles, and the enforcing court's recognition of the chosen law.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Select governing law from a jurisdiction whose judgments are enforceable in all relevant counterparty jurisdictions. Consider Hague Convention coverage.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Favorable judgment obtained but unenforceable in the jurisdiction where the counterparty holds assets.

clause_interactions.description: Language provisions supplement adaptation clauses by establishing which language version prevails when contract amendments or adaptations are negotiated, preventing translation-related disputes.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Designate one language as prevailing in case of conflict. Require certified translations for any adapted provisions in the secondary language.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Contract adaptation creates conflicting obligations in different language versions with no mechanism to determine which prevails.

clause_interactions.description: Local content requirements limit assignment by requiring that certain work or supply percentages be performed by local entities, restricting the ability to assign or subcontract to non-local parties.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify assignment provisions preserve local content compliance. Require assignee to demonstrate local content capability before consent is granted.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Assignment to foreign entity breaches local content requirements, jeopardising the entire contract or government concession.

clause_interactions.description: Sovereign immunity provisions may limit the effectiveness of ICC arbitration by restricting enforcement of arbitral awards against state entities and their assets in certain jurisdictions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Obtain express waiver of sovereign immunity for arbitration purposes including award enforcement. Identify non-immune state commercial assets in enforceable jurisdictions.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Arbitral award against state entity unenforceable due to sovereign immunity defence, rendering the dispute resolution mechanism hollow.

clause_interactions.description: Trade embargo provisions may conflict with force majeure where an embargo is imposed after contract formation, creating a tension between illegality of performance and the force majeure relief mechanism.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Clarify whether trade embargo constitutes force majeure or triggers a separate illegality termination right. Define which party bears the economic loss.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Party seeks force majeure relief for embargo-prevented performance, but counterparty argues the embargoing risk was foreseeable and not a qualifying event.

clause_interactions.description: Anti-money laundering provisions supplement sanctions compliance by establishing customer due diligence, transaction monitoring, and suspicious activity reporting that support sanctions screening.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure AML provisions cover enhanced due diligence for high-risk jurisdictions. Align KYC requirements with sanctions screening obligations.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Sanctions screening detects sanctioned party but AML provisions insufficient to investigate the wider transaction network.

clause_interactions.description: Documentary collection provisions supplement letter of credit arrangements by providing a lower-cost alternative payment mechanism for lower-risk transactions or as a fallback when LC is not required.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define when documentary collection versus letter of credit is required based on transaction value, counterparty risk, and country risk thresholds.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Documentary collection used for high-risk transactions where letter of credit protection was needed, exposing seller to payment default.

clause_interactions.description: Government step-in rights require a performance regime to define the performance failure thresholds that trigger the authority's right to step in and assume service delivery.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define step-in triggers by reference to specific performance regime failures (e.g., persistent KPI breach, critical service failure). Include graduated intervention before full step-in.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Step-in right exists but lacks objective triggers, creating arbitrary exercise risk or inability to step in when genuinely needed.

clause_interactions.description: Government service credits supplement the performance regime by providing financial incentives for performance improvement and compensation for service shortfalls before escalation to step-in or termination.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure service credit levels are proportionate to the impact of performance failures. Check that credit accumulation triggers escalation to formal remediation or step-in.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Service credits too low to incentivise performance improvement, becoming a cost of doing business rather than a genuine performance mechanism.

clause_interactions.description: Open book accounting provisions require public sector audit rights to verify cost declarations, subcontractor margins, and profit levels reported under the transparency mechanism.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure audit rights cover all cost components declared under open book, including subcontractor costs, overheads, and profit margin calculations.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Open book disclosures cannot be verified without corresponding audit rights, making the transparency mechanism unenforceable.

clause_interactions.description: Gain-share/pain-share mechanisms supplement open book accounting by creating shared incentives for cost efficiency, with savings shared between authority and supplier and overruns similarly allocated.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define baseline costs from which gain/pain is measured. Ensure gain-share formula incentivises genuine efficiency rather than initial cost inflation.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Gain-share calculated against inflated baseline, rewarding the supplier for reducing artificially high costs to normal levels.

clause_interactions.description: Freedom of information obligations conflict with mutual confidentiality provisions because public authorities must disclose contract information upon valid FOI request, overriding contractual confidentiality.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Include FOI carve-out in confidentiality clause. Define process for consulting the supplier before FOI disclosure. Identify genuinely commercially sensitive information.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Commercially sensitive information disclosed under FOI where confidentiality clause did not account for statutory disclosure obligations.

clause_interactions.description: Social value commitments supplement sustainability requirements by adding measurable social outcomes (local employment, SME participation, community investment) to environmental sustainability goals.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure social value metrics are SMART (specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, time-bound). Align with the Social Value Act and procurement evaluation criteria.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Social value commitments made at tender stage lack measurable outcomes, becoming unenforceable aspirational statements.

clause_interactions.description: Most favoured customer provisions require benchmarking mechanisms to verify that the government authority receives pricing at least as favourable as the supplier's other comparable customers.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define benchmarking methodology, frequency, and comparator group. Include price reduction mechanism if MFC breach is identified through benchmarking.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: MFC clause is a contractual right without enforcement mechanism because no benchmarking process exists to detect pricing disparities.

clause_interactions.description: TUPE provisions in public contracts require key personnel clauses to ensure that specialist staff critical to service delivery are identified and retained through the TUPE transfer process.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Identify key personnel before TUPE transfer. Ensure retention mechanisms are in place for critical staff. Address key personnel replacement approval process.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Key personnel leave during TUPE transfer due to lack of retention provisions, degrading service quality in the transition period.

clause_interactions.description: Government break clauses limit termination fees by providing the authority with a contractual exit point at reduced or no cost, reflecting the public interest in not being locked into underperforming contracts.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify break clause compensation formula does not effectively replicate full termination fees. Ensure break notice period is commercially reasonable.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Break clause compensation so high it effectively eliminates the break right, defeating the public interest purpose.

clause_interactions.description: Continuous improvement obligations supplement the performance regime by requiring year-on-year service improvement beyond baseline KPIs, preventing suppliers from merely meeting minimum standards.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define measurable improvement targets with annual escalation. Link improvement obligations to service credit adjustments and contract extension eligibility.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Supplier meets minimum performance standards but service quality stagnates over a long-term contract without improvement incentives.

clause_interactions.description: SME subcontracting commitments supplement transparency obligations by requiring visibility into supply chain composition, subcontractor spend, and progress toward SME participation targets.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define SME spend reporting requirements and frequency. Include remediation plans for shortfalls against SME participation commitments.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: SME participation targets not monitored due to insufficient transparency reporting, making the commitment unenforceable.

clause_interactions.description: Conflict of interest provisions require ethical wall arrangements to operationally separate conflicted business units when a supplier serves competing government interests or holds advisory and delivery roles.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define ethical wall requirements including information barriers, separate personnel, and independent reporting lines. Include audit rights over ethical wall compliance.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Conflict of interest declared but no ethical wall implemented, allowing cross-contamination of sensitive government information.

clause_interactions.description: Security clearance requirements limit assignment of government contracts because assignees must obtain equivalent security clearances before accessing classified information or sensitive sites.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Require assignee security clearance as a condition of assignment consent. Define interim arrangements during clearance processing period.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Contract assigned to party without required security clearance, causing service interruption or classified information exposure.

clause_interactions.description: Government data sovereignty provisions require data localisation to ensure that citizen data and government information is stored and processed within the national jurisdiction.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify data localisation covers all processing including backups, disaster recovery, and support access. Address cloud infrastructure geographic placement.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Government data processed offshore despite sovereignty requirements, creating national security risk and regulatory breach.

clause_interactions.description: Innovation clauses supplement continuous improvement by encouraging step-change improvements through technology adoption, process redesign, and emerging solution integration beyond incremental gains.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define innovation proposal process, IP ownership for innovations, and gain-sharing for innovation-driven savings. Include proof-of-concept funding mechanism.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Continuous improvement limited to incremental gains where transformational innovation was needed but not incentivised.

clause_interactions.description: Public procurement provisions require state aid analysis to ensure contract award terms do not constitute unlawful state aid through above-market pricing, exclusive arrangements, or preferential terms.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Conduct state aid assessment for any contract term that provides economic advantage to the supplier beyond normal market conditions.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Contract terms constitute unlawful state aid requiring recovery, destabilising the supplier and disrupting service delivery.

clause_interactions.description: Supply chain transparency provisions require sustainability audit rights to verify disclosed information about supply chain practices, working conditions, and environmental compliance.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure audit rights extend to all tiers of the supply chain, not just direct suppliers. Define unannounced audit provisions for high-risk suppliers.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Supply chain transparency disclosures cannot be verified, creating greenwashing risk and potential CSRD non-compliance.

clause_interactions.description: Modern slavery provisions require human rights due diligence processes to identify, prevent, and mitigate forced labour risks across the supply chain in compliance with modern slavery legislation.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure human rights due diligence scope covers all indicators of forced labour. Include remediation requirements and worker grievance mechanisms.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Modern slavery statement published without operational due diligence, creating legal exposure under the Modern Slavery Act and reputational risk.

clause_interactions.description: Scope 3 emissions obligations require carbon disclosure provisions to ensure supply chain partners provide accurate emissions data for the reporting entity's value chain calculations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define carbon disclosure methodology (GHG Protocol), reporting frequency, and data quality requirements. Include provision for third-party verification.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Scope 3 reporting obligations unmet because supply chain carbon disclosure provisions are insufficient or non-existent.

clause_interactions.description: Greenwashing liability provisions conflict with green claims clauses where environmental claims made in the contract or marketing materials cannot be substantiated, creating liability for misleading sustainability representations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure all green claims are substantiated with verifiable data and comply with the EU Green Claims Directive. Include indemnification for unsubstantiated claims.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Green claims in contract materials trigger greenwashing liability due to insufficient substantiation or misleading environmental representations.

clause_interactions.description: ESG termination triggers supplement sustainability audits by providing contractual consequences when audit findings reveal material ESG non-compliance, escalating beyond remediation to contract exit.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define ESG breaches that trigger termination versus remediation. Include graduated response (warning, remediation plan, termination) with clear timelines.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Sustainability audits reveal serious ESG violations but contract lacks termination trigger, trapping the party in a reputationally damaging relationship.

clause_interactions.description: Child labour prohibition clauses require supply chain transparency to monitor compliance across all tiers, particularly in industries and geographies with elevated child labour risk.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure transparency extends to raw material sourcing and sub-tier suppliers in high-risk sectors. Include age verification and school attendance monitoring.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Child labour prohibition clause exists but no visibility into supply chain operations to detect violations.

clause_interactions.description: Conflict minerals provisions require supply chain transparency to trace mineral sourcing to smelter/refiner level and verify that tin, tantalum, tungsten, and gold do not finance armed conflict.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Require CMRT (Conflict Minerals Reporting Template) completion. Verify smelter-level traceability and RMI (Responsible Minerals Initiative) conformance.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Conflict minerals commitment unverifiable due to insufficient supply chain traceability, exposing the company to Dodd-Frank or EU Conflict Minerals Regulation liability.

clause_interactions.description: Deforestation-free provisions supplement biodiversity commitments by specifically addressing forest conversion and land use change, which are major drivers of biodiversity loss.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define deforestation-free cut-off date and verification methodology. Ensure alignment with EU Deforestation Regulation requirements and geolocation tracing.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Biodiversity commitment does not address deforestation-driven habitat loss, the largest single cause of biodiversity decline.

clause_interactions.description: Circular economy provisions supplement environmental remediation by addressing waste prevention, material recovery, and product lifecycle design that reduce the need for end-of-life remediation.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define circular economy targets (recycled content, recyclability, take-back obligations) alongside traditional remediation requirements.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Environmental remediation costs increase because circular economy principles were not applied to reduce waste generation at source.

clause_interactions.description: Living wage provisions supplement labour standards by establishing a higher wage floor than legal minimums, addressing in-work poverty and demonstrating commitment to fair employment practices.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define living wage calculation methodology (e.g., Living Wage Foundation rates). Specify whether living wage applies to subcontractor and agency workers.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Labour standards commitment undermined by paying legal minimum rather than living wage, creating reputational inconsistency.

clause_interactions.description: ESG reporting obligations under CSRD, TCFD, or SEC climate rules require carbon disclosure from supply chain partners to compile complete and accurate sustainability reports.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Align carbon disclosure requirements with applicable reporting framework (CSRD, TCFD, ISSB). Define data format, frequency, and assurance level.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: ESG reporting obligations unmet due to missing supply chain carbon data, triggering regulatory enforcement and investor concerns.

clause_interactions.description: Just transition provisions supplement modern slavery commitments by ensuring that decarbonisation and green transition activities do not create new forms of labour exploitation or displacement.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure just transition covers worker retraining, fair redundancy terms, and community impact mitigation for workers displaced by green transition.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Green transition celebrated as environmental progress while creating labour exploitation in new supply chains (e.g., mining for battery minerals).

clause_interactions.description: Water stewardship provisions supplement environmental remediation by establishing proactive water management obligations including consumption reduction, pollution prevention, and watershed protection.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define water stewardship standards by reference to AWS (Alliance for Water Stewardship) or CDP Water Security. Include water stress area analysis.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Environmental remediation addresses contamination after the fact while water stewardship provisions that would prevent contamination are absent.

clause_interactions.description: Human rights due diligence supplements labour standards by extending protections beyond employment law compliance to cover broader human rights impacts including community rights and indigenous peoples.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure HRDD scope extends beyond direct labour to cover land rights, community impacts, and access to remedy. Align with UNGPs and OECD Guidelines.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Labour standards compliance achieved but broader human rights violations (community displacement, indigenous rights) unaddressed.

clause_interactions.description: Automated decision-making provisions require privacy impact assessments under GDPR Article 35 when AI processes personal data to make decisions that produce legal or similarly significant effects on individuals.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure DPIA is completed before AI automated decision-making goes live. Include bias assessment as part of the DPIA for high-risk AI processing.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: AI automated decisions processed without mandatory DPIA, creating GDPR enforcement exposure and potential prohibition of processing.

clause_interactions.description: AI training data rights require data processing instructions to define lawful bases, purposes, and restrictions for using personal data in model training under the DPA framework.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure processing instructions explicitly address AI training as a processing purpose. Define data minimisation requirements for training datasets.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Personal data used for AI training without lawful processing instructions, creating GDPR breach for both controller and processor.

clause_interactions.description: Modern slavery provisions require anti-bribery compliance because corruption and bribery in supply chains are enablers of forced labour, particularly in jurisdictions with weak enforcement.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Cross-reference anti-bribery and modern slavery due diligence processes. Flag suppliers in high corruption AND high forced labour risk jurisdictions for enhanced scrutiny.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Modern slavery due diligence misses corruption-enabled forced labour because anti-bribery and modern slavery programmes operate in silos.

clause_interactions.description: Construction delay damages provisions require the financial liquidated damages framework to establish pre-agreed rates representing genuine pre-estimates of the employer's loss from late completion.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure delay damages rate represents a genuine pre-estimate of loss. Document the calculation methodology to defend against penalty challenge.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Delay damages struck down as penalty because no genuine pre-estimate of loss calculation was documented or rates are disproportionate.

clause_interactions.description: M&A compliance warranties require ESG reporting verification because CSRD, TCFD, and other sustainability reporting obligations are increasingly material to transaction value and risk assessment.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Include ESG reporting compliance as a specific warranty. Conduct ESG due diligence covering reporting accuracy, data quality, and pending regulatory changes.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Compliance warranty satisfied but ESG reporting deficiencies discovered post-acquisition, requiring restatement and creating regulatory exposure.

clause_interactions.description: Government performance regimes for digital services require SLA uptime commitments to define measurable availability targets that feed into the broader performance measurement framework.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure SLA uptime targets align with government service availability expectations (typically 99.9%+ for critical services). Define measurement methodology.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Government performance regime references uptime without specific SLA targets, making performance measurement subjective.

clause_interactions.description: DORA exit strategy provisions for financial institutions require AI-specific cloud exit planning when AI services are hosted by third-party cloud providers, ensuring continuity of critical AI functions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify AI cloud exit plan addresses model portability, training data export, inference pipeline migration, and service continuity during transition.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: DORA exit strategy covers general IT services but fails to address AI-specific migration challenges, creating regulatory non-compliance.

clause_interactions.description: Employee IP assignment provisions supplement M&A IP warranties by ensuring that all IP created by employees is properly assigned to the company, supporting the seller's warranty that it owns the transferred IP.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Audit employee IP assignment agreements as part of M&A IP due diligence. Flag any gaps in assignment coverage, particularly for pre-incorporation IP.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: M&A IP warranty breached because employee IP assignments were incomplete, leaving key IP owned by individual employees rather than the company.

clause_interactions.description: Sanctions compliance requirements limit M&A conditions precedent by potentially blocking regulatory approvals or prohibiting completion where target entities have sanctions exposure.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Include comprehensive sanctions screening in M&A due diligence. Define sanctions discovery as a condition precedent failure trigger or MAC event.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: M&A completion blocked at final stage due to sanctions exposure discovered after conditions precedent were substantially satisfied.

clause_interactions.description: NIS2 security requirements supplement AI penetration testing by providing the regulatory framework for security testing of essential and important entity AI systems under the EU cybersecurity directive.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure AI penetration testing meets NIS2 security requirements for essential/important entities. Include supply chain security testing per NIS2 Article 21.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: AI penetration testing conducted without NIS2 alignment, missing regulatory requirements for essential service security testing.

clause_interactions.description: Contractor design portion provisions require IP work product ownership clauses to determine who owns the design IP when the contractor creates bespoke designs under the building contract.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Clarify whether design IP transfers to employer or is licensed. Define license scope for employer's use beyond the specific project.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Contractor retains design IP preventing the employer from modifying, extending, or reproducing the designed works without further consent.

clause_interactions.description: Scope 3 emissions requirements supplement construction sustainability provisions by mandating carbon measurement and reduction across the construction supply chain including materials, transport, and waste.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define Scope 3 measurement methodology for construction (embodied carbon calculation). Include whole-life carbon assessment requirements.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Construction sustainability addresses operational energy but ignores embodied carbon in materials and construction processes.

clause_interactions.description: AI data residency provisions require data localisation clauses to ensure that AI training data, model weights, and inference data remain within the specified geographic jurisdiction.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify data localisation covers all AI data categories: training data, fine-tuning data, prompt logs, model weights, and inference outputs.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: AI data residency commitment violated because data localisation provisions did not cover all AI-specific data categories.

clause_interactions.description: DORA incident reporting supplements general breach notification by imposing additional financial sector-specific reporting requirements for ICT-related incidents, with stricter timelines and content requirements.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Map DORA incident reporting timelines against GDPR breach notification requirements. Ensure dual reporting processes are coordinated to avoid conflicting disclosures.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: GDPR breach notification complied with but DORA incident reporting obligations missed, creating financial regulatory enforcement exposure.

clause_interactions.description: M&A change of control provisions may be limited by government security clearance requirements where the acquirer must obtain security clearance before taking control of cleared operations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Identify government contracts requiring security clearance in M&A due diligence. Obtain clearance pre-approval or structure completion to preserve cleared operations.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Acquisition completes but government contracts terminated because acquirer lacks required security clearance, destroying acquired revenue base.

clause_interactions.description: TUPE transfer provisions supplement construction contract novation by ensuring employee rights are protected when construction contracts are novated from one contractor to another.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Confirm whether contract novation triggers TUPE. Include employee liability apportionment and information obligations in the novation agreement.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Construction novation proceeds without TUPE compliance, creating automatic unfair dismissal claims from transferred employees.

clause_interactions.description: Responsible AI provisions supplement ESG reporting by providing the AI governance framework data needed for sustainability reports covering AI ethics, bias, and social impact metrics.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Include AI governance metrics in ESG reporting scope. Map responsible AI KPIs to CSRD disclosure requirements for artificial intelligence systems.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: ESG reporting omits AI governance disclosures, creating incomplete sustainability reporting as AI becomes material to operations.

clause_interactions.description: International cross-border data transfer provisions require Standard Contractual Clauses (Module 2, Controller to Processor) when personal data is transferred to processors in countries without adequacy decisions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify SCC module selection matches the actual data transfer scenario. Ensure SCCs are supplemented by transfer impact assessment and technical measures.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Cross-border data transfers proceed without required SCCs, creating GDPR Chapter V violation and potential transfer prohibition.

clause_interactions.description: Sovereign immunity provisions conflict with financial step-in rights because state entities may invoke immunity to prevent lenders from exercising step-in rights over government-contracted services.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Obtain express sovereign immunity waiver for step-in purposes. Ensure waiver covers both enforcement proceedings and direct step-in actions.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Lender step-in right unexercisable due to sovereign immunity, undermining project finance security package.

clause_interactions.description: ESG sustainability audits supplement operational audit rights by extending audit scope to cover environmental compliance, labour practices, and governance standards beyond traditional operational metrics.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Include ESG audit scope in operational audit rights framework. Define ESG-specific audit standards, qualified auditor requirements, and remediation timelines.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Operational audits conducted without ESG coverage, missing material sustainability risks in supplier operations.

clause_interactions.description: Construction-specific modern slavery provisions supplement general ESG modern slavery clauses by addressing industry-specific risks including labour supply chain agency workers, subcontractor cascading, and site accommodation standards.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure construction modern slavery provisions cover agency labour checks, right-to-work verification, and accommodation standards specific to construction sites.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: General modern slavery clause does not address construction-specific exploitation patterns (gangmaster labour, worker accommodation, document retention).

clause_interactions.description: AI liability allocation provisions supplement provider indemnification by defining the specific scenarios in which AI-related harms trigger provider indemnity obligations versus customer responsibility.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Map AI liability scenarios (model error, data quality, integration failure) to the indemnification framework. Define shared liability scenarios clearly.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: AI incident occurs but liability allocation and indemnification provisions point to different responsible parties, creating dispute.

clause_interactions.description: Minimum insurance coverage requirements specify the floor for professional indemnity insurance that construction professionals must maintain throughout the contract and survival period.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify PI coverage levels match the minimum requirements. Check that coverage is maintained on each-and-every-claim basis, not aggregate, for multi-project exposure.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: PI insurance in place but below contractual minimum, leaving a coverage gap for professional negligence claims.

clause_interactions.description: DORA security testing requirements supplement general security audit rights by imposing threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) obligations for critical ICT service providers to financial entities.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure security audit rights permit DORA-compliant TLPT methodology (TIBER-EU). Coordinate testing schedules to avoid service disruption.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: General security audit rights insufficient for DORA TLPT requirements, creating regulatory compliance gap for financial sector clients.

clause_interactions.description: Data breach indemnification requires adequate security measures to be specified, as the indemnity scope and triggering conditions depend on the security standard the processor committed to maintain.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that security measures are defined with sufficient specificity that breach of those measures clearly triggers the indemnification obligation.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Data breach occurs but indemnification claim fails because security obligations were too vague to establish breach of duty.

clause_interactions.description: Wind-down provisions must incorporate data deletion and return obligations to ensure personal data is handled in accordance with GDPR requirements upon contract termination.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that the wind-down timeline allows sufficient time for data return in a usable format before deletion. Define data formats and verification procedures.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Wind-down period expires with data still held by processor, creating ongoing GDPR non-compliance after contract termination.

clause_interactions.description: Traditional IP work product ownership clauses may conflict with AI output ownership provisions when deliverables are generated or substantially assisted by AI tools, creating ambiguity about which regime applies.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define the boundary between human-authored work product and AI-generated output. Specify which ownership regime applies to hybrid deliverables.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Ownership dispute where work product was created using AI assistance and both traditional IP and AI output clauses claim to govern ownership.

clause_interactions.description: General mutual confidentiality must be complemented by DPA-specific personnel confidentiality obligations to satisfy GDPR Article 28(3)(b) requirements for persons authorised to process personal data.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that DPA confidentiality extends to all personnel categories (permanent, temporary, subcontractors) and is documented through individual undertakings or contractual terms.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: General NDA in place but GDPR-specific personnel confidentiality obligations unmet, creating processor non-compliance.

clause_interactions.description: Broad force majeure definitions may effectively nullify SLA uptime commitments by excusing performance for events that should have been planned for in a high-availability architecture.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Narrow force majeure to truly unforeseeable events. Exclude infrastructure failures, provider outages, and routine IT incidents that uptime SLAs are designed to address.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Provider invokes force majeure for cloud provider outages, effectively reducing real-world uptime below the contracted SLA target.

clause_interactions.description: General security audit rights must be coordinated with DPA-specific audit rights to ensure a comprehensive audit scope that covers both operational security and GDPR-mandated processor oversight.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Harmonise audit schedules and scope between general security audits and DPA audits to avoid duplication and ensure complete coverage.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Separate audit regimes create gaps where some security measures are never audited or where DPA audit scope is narrower than operational security audit.

clause_interactions.description: Termination for cause provisions may conflict with force majeure excuses where the non-performing party claims force majeure to avoid termination for what appears to be a material breach.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define whether force majeure excuses a material breach that would otherwise constitute termination cause. Set time limits beyond which force majeure converts to a termination right.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Party in material breach invokes force majeure defence to prevent termination, trapping the other party in a non-performing contract.

clause_interactions.description: AI model governance frameworks supplement DORA security testing by adding model-specific testing requirements such as adversarial robustness testing, model drift detection, and AI-specific vulnerability assessment.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure DORA security testing scope includes AI model-specific tests. Map model governance testing requirements to DORA TLPT methodology.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: DORA security testing covers ICT infrastructure but misses AI model-specific vulnerabilities, creating a regulatory compliance gap for AI-based financial services.

clause_interactions.description: ESG reporting obligations under CSRD may conflict with mutual confidentiality provisions when sustainability disclosures require revealing supply chain data, pricing structures, or operational practices that are otherwise confidential.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Include ESG disclosure carve-out in confidentiality clause. Define the scope of information that may be disclosed for ESG reporting purposes without breaching confidentiality.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Party unable to meet mandatory ESG reporting requirements because supply chain data is classified as confidential under the mutual NDA.

clause_interactions.description: AI content filtering provisions supplement accuracy warranties by establishing technical safeguards that support the warranty that AI-generated outputs meet accuracy and quality standards.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Align content filtering thresholds with accuracy warranty standards. Ensure filtering catches factual errors, hallucinations, and misleading content before delivery.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Accuracy warranty exists but content filtering is insufficient to prevent inaccurate AI outputs, leading to frequent warranty breach claims.

clause_interactions.description: ESG supply chain transparency supplements NIS2 supply chain security requirements by extending transparency beyond cybersecurity to cover environmental, social, and governance dimensions of the supply chain.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Coordinate ESG and NIS2 supply chain reporting to avoid duplication. Use a single supply chain mapping exercise covering both cybersecurity and ESG risk dimensions.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Separate supply chain transparency processes create inconsistent supplier risk profiles and duplicative reporting burden.

clause_interactions.description: AI human oversight provisions require termination for cause triggers in case the AI provider persistently fails to implement or maintain the agreed human oversight mechanisms, particularly for high-risk AI systems.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define failure to maintain human oversight as a material breach triggering termination for cause. Include graduated remediation before termination.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Provider removes or degrades human oversight mechanisms but contract lacks termination right for this specific material breach.

clause_interactions.description: Sub-processor engagement may conflict with data localisation requirements when sub-processors are located in or process data through jurisdictions outside the specified data localisation territory.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Require sub-processor data localisation compliance as a condition of sub-processor approval. Map all sub-processor processing locations against localisation requirements.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Sub-processor processes data outside the localisation territory, violating data residency requirements despite compliant primary processor.

clause_interactions.description: AI algorithmic audit provisions supplement general regulatory audit rights by adding AI-specific audit methodologies including bias testing, fairness assessment, and model performance evaluation under the EU AI Act framework.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure regulatory audit rights explicitly include algorithmic audit scope. Define audit methodology standards (e.g., ISO/IEC 42001) and auditor qualifications for AI systems.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Regulatory audit conducted without AI-specific methodology, missing algorithmic bias, model drift, and AI-specific compliance issues.

clause_interactions.description: General liability cap may limit the cumulative recovery of liquidated damages if LDs are not carved out from the cap, potentially leaving the non-breaching party under-compensated for repeated breaches.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Clarify whether liquidated damages count toward the general liability cap or are a separate remedy. Consider carving out LDs from the cap up to a separate LD cap.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Liquidated damages erode the general liability cap, reducing capacity for other claims, or LD accumulation is inadvertently unlimited.

clause_interactions.description: Green claims provisions may conflict with Scope 3 emissions reduction commitments if environmental marketing claims rely on carbon offsets rather than genuine supply chain decarbonisation, undermining science-based targets.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure green claims are substantiated by actual emissions reduction, not merely offset purchases. Require offset claims to be limited to residual emissions with Gold Standard or equivalent certification.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Organisation markets net-zero claims through offsets while actual Scope 3 emissions are increasing, creating greenwashing exposure under the EU Green Claims Directive.

clause_interactions.description: Foundation model licence agreements require AI output indemnification provisions to allocate liability for IP infringement, defamation, or other harms caused by outputs generated using the licensed model.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify foundation model provider offers output indemnity covering at least IP infringement claims. Assess whether indemnity scope matches the intended use cases.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Foundation model used commercially without output indemnity, leaving the customer exposed to third-party IP claims from AI-generated content.

clause_interactions.description: Construction adjudication supplements escalation procedures by providing a statutory rapid dispute resolution mechanism that operates independently of contractual escalation tiers.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Clarify that the statutory right to adjudication is preserved regardless of contractual escalation requirements. Ensure escalation does not create a precondition to adjudication.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Party argues adjudication is premature because contractual escalation was not exhausted, potentially delaying the statutory right to adjudication.

clause_interactions.description: Government environmental requirements supplement circular economy provisions by adding public procurement-specific waste reduction, recycled content, and end-of-life product management obligations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Align circular economy targets with government environmental evaluation criteria. Ensure commitments made at tender are enforceable through the contract lifecycle.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Circular economy commitments in tender response are not reflected in enforceable contractual obligations, creating compliance gaps during delivery.

clause_interactions.description: Services warranty breaches need to be connected to termination for cause rights to provide a meaningful remedy when services consistently fail to meet the warranted standard of care.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define persistent warranty breach (e.g., 3+ breaches in 12 months) as a termination trigger. Include cure period before termination right arises.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Services consistently fail to meet warranted quality but customer has no termination right specific to persistent warranty breach.

clause_interactions.description: AI data portability provisions may conflict with mutual confidentiality when exporting data to a new provider requires disclosing information that the exporting provider considers confidential, including model configurations and integration details.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Include data portability carve-out in confidentiality clause. Define what migration information the customer is entitled to receive despite confidentiality.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Data portability right exists but provider blocks migration by classifying essential export data as confidential information.

clause_interactions.description: Human rights due diligence obligations require operational audit rights to conduct on-site inspections of working conditions, verify labour practices, and assess compliance with human rights commitments at operational facilities.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure audit rights include unannounced site visits, worker interviews without management presence, and access to payroll and employment records.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Human rights due diligence obligations exist but cannot be operationalised because audit rights do not cover social and labour conditions.

clause_interactions.description: Earn-out provisions in M&A transactions conflict with convenience termination rights when the buyer can terminate key revenue-generating contracts, directly reducing earn-out achievement.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Include ordinary course of business covenant preventing buyer from taking actions that materially reduce earn-out metrics. Define anti-avoidance protections.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Buyer terminates or restructures key contracts during earn-out period, reducing target performance and minimising earn-out payments.

clause_interactions.description: DORA business continuity arrangements for financial entities require cyber insurance to ensure financial resources are available for incident response, recovery operations, and regulatory compliance during ICT disruptions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify cyber insurance covers DORA-specific continuity obligations including third-party ICT service disruptions and supply chain incidents.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: DORA continuity plan exists but cyber insurance does not cover the costs of executing the plan, creating a funding gap during ICT incidents.

clause_interactions.description: Country of origin provisions require customs compliance to ensure origin determinations are correctly documented, preferential tariff claims are substantiated, and anti-dumping duties are properly assessed.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify origin documentation (certificates of origin, supplier declarations) aligns with customs compliance procedures. Address origin changes from substantial transformation.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Incorrect origin declaration leads to customs penalties, loss of preferential tariff rates, or anti-dumping duty liability.

clause_interactions.description: Disciplinary procedures require complementary grievance procedures to ensure employees have a channel to raise concerns about the disciplinary process itself, supporting procedural fairness and ACAS compliance.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure both procedures exist and cross-reference each other. Verify grievance procedure covers complaints about disciplinary decisions and that both comply with the ACAS Code of Practice.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Disciplinary action taken without adequate grievance mechanism, resulting in procedurally unfair dismissal and increased tribunal compensation.

clause_interactions.description: AI model version updates require SLA uptime provisions to define the permitted downtime for model deployments, rollbacks, and version transitions without breaching availability commitments.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Include model version deployment in SLA maintenance window definition. Define rollback SLA for failed model updates to minimise service disruption.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Model version deployment causes service outage that breaches SLA because uptime provisions did not account for AI model update requirements.

clause_interactions.description: Asset transfer provisions upon contract termination require wind-down arrangements to coordinate the timing, valuation, and physical transfer of customer-owned or customer-funded assets held by the provider.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define asset inventory, condition assessment, and transfer logistics within the wind-down timeline. Address asset depreciation and residual value calculation.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Contract terminates but customer assets remain with provider because no wind-down process addresses asset identification, valuation, and physical transfer.

clause_interactions.description: Supply chain transparency commitments require specific conflict minerals provisions to address the due diligence obligations for tin, tantalum, tungsten, and gold under the EU Conflict Minerals Regulation and Dodd-Frank Section 1502.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure supply chain transparency framework explicitly includes CMRT reporting and RMI conformant smelter requirements for products containing 3TG minerals.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Supply chain transparency in place but specific conflict minerals due diligence absent, failing to meet regulatory requirements for 3TG mineral sourcing.

clause_interactions.description: AI bias monitoring provisions supplement equal pay obligations when AI systems are used in compensation decisions, ensuring algorithmic recommendations do not introduce or perpetuate pay disparities across protected characteristics.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Extend AI bias monitoring to cover compensation-related algorithms including performance scoring, bonus allocation, and pay band recommendations.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: AI-assisted compensation decisions create systematic pay disparities that violate equal pay obligations because bias monitoring did not cover HR algorithms.

clause_interactions.description: SLA response time commitments supplement DORA incident reporting by ensuring ICT service providers respond within timeframes that enable the financial entity to meet its regulatory reporting deadlines.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Align SLA response times with DORA incident reporting windows. Ensure P1 response time allows the financial entity sufficient time to assess, classify, and report incidents.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Provider SLA response time exceeds DORA incident reporting window, causing the financial entity to miss mandatory regulatory notification deadlines.

clause_interactions.description: Construction site access provisions require health and safety compliance as a precondition to site entry, ensuring all personnel meet induction, PPE, and competency requirements before accessing the works.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify site access conditions include mandatory H&S induction, site-specific risk assessment, and CSCS card or equivalent competency verification.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Site access granted without health and safety preconditions, exposing the principal contractor to CDM enforcement action.

clause_interactions.description: M&A locked box mechanisms supplement financial price lock provisions by fixing the economic point of transfer at a historical date, with leakage protections ensuring no value extraction between the locked box date and completion.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define permitted leakage categories precisely. Ensure price lock and locked box dates are aligned and that permitted payments during the gap period are exhaustively listed.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Locked box value eroded through undisclosed leakage payments not covered by the price lock or permitted leakage schedule.

clause_interactions.description: AI automated decision-making may conflict with HIPAA permitted use restrictions when AI models process protected health information beyond the scope of treatment, payment, or healthcare operations purposes.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Map AI automated decision use cases against HIPAA permitted use categories. Ensure AI processing does not expand PHI usage beyond the minimum necessary standard.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: AI system processes PHI for automated decisions outside HIPAA permitted uses, creating dual regulatory exposure under both HIPAA and GDPR.

clause_interactions.description: Confidentiality survival provisions supplement IP work product ownership by ensuring trade secrets and proprietary information embedded in assigned work product remain protected after contract termination.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Align confidentiality survival period with the commercially useful life of the IP. Trade secrets should be protected indefinitely; other confidential IP for 5-7 years minimum.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: IP ownership transfers but confidentiality expires, allowing former provider to disclose proprietary aspects of the assigned work product.

clause_interactions.description: Government key personnel requirements depend on underlying non-compete provisions to ensure identified critical staff cannot be recruited by competing suppliers during the contract term.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify key personnel employment agreements include non-compete restrictions covering the contract period. Address security-cleared personnel with additional retention measures.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Key personnel identified in government contract leave to join a competitor, and employer has no non-compete restriction to prevent it.

clause_interactions.description: General financial escrow arrangements supplement AI-specific technology escrow by providing the commercial framework (escrow agent, fees, release mechanics) that underpins the technical escrow of model weights, source code, and training pipelines.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure financial escrow terms cover AI-specific deposit requirements including periodic model weight updates, training data snapshots, and deployment configuration.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: AI technology escrow agreed but financial escrow does not fund regular deposit updates, leaving escrow contents stale and unusable.

clause_interactions.description: Biodiversity monitoring provisions require privacy impact assessments when biodiversity data collection involves location tracking, drone surveillance, or environmental sensors that may inadvertently capture personal data.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Conduct DPIA where biodiversity monitoring technologies process location data or imagery that could identify individuals. Implement data minimisation for environmental monitoring systems.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Biodiversity monitoring captures personal data through geolocation or imagery without DPIA, creating GDPR enforcement risk.

clause_interactions.description: Dispute adjudication boards in FIDIC-type contracts supplement final arbitration by providing standing, real-time dispute resolution during project execution, with DAB decisions becoming binding unless referred to arbitration within the prescribed period.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define DAB decision enforcement mechanism and the timeframe for referral to arbitration. Ensure binding DAB decisions are complied with pending final arbitration.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: DAB decision ignored because the relationship between DAB and final arbitration is ambiguous, losing the benefit of real-time dispute resolution.

clause_interactions.description: International mediation supplements ICC arbitration by providing a cost-effective settlement opportunity before formal arbitration proceedings, particularly valuable in cross-border disputes where cultural differences may exacerbate conflict.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Include mediation as a pre-condition to arbitration with a defined time limit (45-60 days). Specify ICC ADR Rules or CEDR mediation rules as applicable.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Mandatory mediation without time limit delays access to arbitration indefinitely, or mediation omitted entirely, losing settlement opportunity.

clause_interactions.description: Employee social media policies supplement mutual confidentiality by addressing the specific risk of inadvertent disclosure through social media posts, online discussions, and professional networking platform activity.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure social media policy explicitly prohibits disclosure of confidential information including client names, project details, and proprietary methodologies on social platforms.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Confidentiality obligations breached through employee social media posts that the general NDA did not specifically address.

clause_interactions.description: Responsible AI commitments require operational human oversight mechanisms to ensure that AI systems operate within ethical boundaries and that humans can intervene when AI behaviour deviates from responsible AI principles.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Map responsible AI principles to specific human oversight controls. Ensure oversight mechanisms include real-time intervention capability, not just post-hoc review.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Responsible AI commitments are aspirational without operational human oversight to detect and correct AI behaviour that violates those principles.

clause_interactions.description: Export control regulations may limit AI training data rights when training datasets contain controlled technical data, dual-use technology information, or data subject to deemed export restrictions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Screen AI training datasets for export-controlled content before cross-border training or cloud-based processing. Obtain necessary export licences for controlled data used in model training.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: AI model trained on export-controlled data transferred to non-authorised jurisdiction, creating criminal export control violation.

clause_interactions.description: SLA exclusions may conflict with DORA business continuity requirements when excluded events (planned maintenance, force majeure) are precisely the scenarios where financial entities require ICT service continuity.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Narrow SLA exclusions for DORA-regulated clients. Ensure planned maintenance windows comply with DORA continuity requirements for critical and important functions.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: SLA exclusions effectively exempt the provider from continuity obligations during the exact scenarios that DORA requires financial entities to address.

clause_interactions.description: International hardship clauses supplement CPI price adjustment by covering extraordinary economic disruptions that go beyond normal inflationary price movements, such as hyperinflation, currency collapse, or commodity price shocks.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define hardship triggers as economic disruptions beyond the range of normal CPI adjustment. Specify the renegotiation procedure and interim performance obligations during hardship.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: CPI adjustment covers normal inflation but extraordinary economic shock falls outside both CPI mechanism and force majeure, leaving no contractual remedy.

clause_interactions.description: M&A warranty on material contracts requires verification of DORA exit strategy compliance for financial sector targets, ensuring ICT service contracts include the mandatory exit provisions that DORA requires.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Include DORA exit strategy compliance as a specific element of material contracts warranty for financial sector acquisitions. Assess transition risk for non-compliant ICT contracts.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Post-acquisition discovery that target company ICT contracts lack DORA-compliant exit strategies, requiring costly contract renegotiation under regulatory pressure.

clause_interactions.description: Bonus deferral provisions supplement clawback by retaining deferred amounts that can be forfeited upon clawback trigger events, which is operationally simpler than recovering already-paid sums.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Align deferral periods with clawback trigger identification windows. Ensure deferred amounts are subject to the same clawback triggers as paid amounts.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Bonus paid immediately without deferral, making clawback recovery difficult in practice even though clawback right exists contractually.

clause_interactions.description: Government step-in rights require security clearance provisions for replacement personnel, ensuring that staff deployed during step-in hold the required clearance levels for the service being stepped into.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify step-in plans include clearance requirements for replacement personnel. Define interim arrangements if cleared staff are not immediately available for step-in deployment.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Step-in right exercised but replacement personnel cannot access classified information or secure environments because clearance requirements were not addressed in step-in planning.

clause_interactions.description: AI API-specific SLAs supplement general response time commitments by defining inference-specific latency targets, token throughput rates, and batch processing times that go beyond standard API response metrics.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Define AI-specific response metrics: inference latency at P50/P95/P99, tokens per second throughput, and batch job completion targets. Separate from standard HTTP response time.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: General SLA response time met but AI inference latency unacceptable because AI-specific performance metrics were not separately defined.

clause_interactions.description: Data minimisation obligations under the DPA limit AI training data rights by restricting the volume and categories of personal data that can be used for model training to what is strictly necessary for the specified processing purpose.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure AI training data rights are qualified by data minimisation principles. Define the minimum dataset required for effective model training and prohibit retention of excess data.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: AI model trained on excessive personal data volumes that violate data minimisation principles, creating GDPR enforcement exposure and potential regulatory fines.
